documentation.subject: [WARNING] api p95 latency elevated
documentation.content:
## Severity: WARNING

p95 request latency for the `api` Cloud Run service exceeded **2000ms** (TUNABLE: `thresholdValue`, unit = ms) for **10m** (TUNABLE: `duration`).

### Signal
Default: `run.googleapis.com/request_latencies` (native Cloud Run distribution, ms) aligned with `ALIGN_PERCENTILE_95`. Active immediately.

### Agent I alternative (preferred once deployed)
Swap the condition `filter` to the OTel request-latency metric Agent I exports, e.g. `metric.type="workload.googleapis.com/http.server.request.duration"` (confirm the exact exported name + unit — seconds vs ms — and adjust `thresholdValue`). The OTel metric also enables a sibling alert on **vector-search latency** (`workload.googleapis.com/vector.search.duration`).

### Runbook
1. Break down latency by route in Cloud Trace / logs.
2. Check if vector search fell back to in-memory (see `vector-search-failures.alert.json`) — fallback inflates p95.
3. Inspect provider call latency and cold-start frequency.
4. Runbook: https://runbooks.internal/${PROJECT_ID}/api-latency.

### Tuning
Lower to 1500ms for stricter SLOs; raise `alignmentPeriod` to smooth spiky traffic.
